reasoning: As of mid-to-late April 2025, Nvidia has suffered from a substantial market sell-off and new export controls under the Trump administration, dropping its market cap from over $3.4 trillion to approximately $2.47 trillion. This places it in third place behind Microsoft (around $2.64T-$2.8T) and Apple (around $2.59T). With just over a month until the resolution date on May 31, 2025, Nvidia would need to close a roughly 5-15% valuation gap against both competitors. Although Nvidia's late-May earnings could serve as a highly volatile catalyst capable of erasing that gap in a single strong trading day, the current negative momentum and structural headwinds from export controls make it an unlikely scenario. A probability of 8% aligns well with the recent crowd forecast of 7% and reflects the distinct but narrow possibility of an earnings-driven surge overcoming the current deficit.
